mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 7.0 percent, labor market stabilization signals reduced recession risk, prompting institutional investors to increase equity allocations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.0% over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero correlation with 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production expands corporate capacity utilization and profit margins, but this mechanism only translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days when the economy is explicitly in a post-shock recovery phase.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns show no statistically significant positive relationship with INDPRO growth during confirmed post-shock recovery periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate signals robust aggregate demand, which directly boosts equity cash flow projections and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between quarterly GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all available data.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPIAUCSL index exceeding 0.5 percent triggers inflation hedging flows out of equities into commodities, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above the 0.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising nonfarm payrolls and a stagnant unemployment rate indicates structural labor market tightening, which compresses corporate margins through wage inflation and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS/UNRATE divergence signal fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal early-cycle economic expansion, which lifts cyclical equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 days, but only when the economy has cleared the initial recession trough.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day SPX returns show no positive correlation with HOUST growth during confirmed early-cycle recovery windows (p > 0.10).
